2.text:
TC1263
Features Description
• Very Low Dropout Voltage The TC1263 is a fixed-output, high-accuracy (typically
• 500mA Output Current ±0.5%) CMOS low dropout regulator. Designed
specifically for battery-operated systems, the TC1263’s
• High-Output Voltage Accuracy
CMOS construction eliminates wasted ground current,
• Standard or Custom Output Voltages
significantly extending battery life. Total supply current
• Overcurrent and Overtemperature Protection
is typically 80µA at full load (20 to 60 times lower than
• SHDN Input for Active Power Management in bipolar regulators).
• ERROR Output Can Be Used as a Low Battery
TC1263 key features include ultra low noise operation,
Detector (SOIC only)
very low dropout voltage (typically 350mV at full load)
and fast response to step changes in load.
Applications
The TC1263 incorporates both overtemperature and
• Battery-Operated Systems overcurrent protection. The TC1263 is stable with an
output capacitor of only 1µF and has a maximum
• Portable Computers
output current of 500mA. It is available in 8-Pin SOIC,
• Medical Instruments
5-Pin TO-220 and 5-Pin DDPAK packages.
• Instrumentation
• Cellular/GSM/PHS Phones Package Type
• Linear Post-Regulators for SMPS
• Pagers
Typical Application

4: Regulation is measured at a constant junction temperature using low duty-cycle pulse testing. Load regulation is tested
over a load range from 0.1mA to the maximum specified output current. Changes in output voltage due to heating
effects are covered by the thermal regulation specification.
5: Dropout voltage is defined as the input-to-output differential at which the output voltage drops 2% below its nominal
value measured at a 1.0V differential.
6: Thermal regulation is defined as the change in output voltage at a time T after a change in power dissipation is applied,
excluding load or line regulation effects. Specifications are for a current pulse equal to I at V = 6V for T = 10ms.
LMAX IN
7: The maximum allowable power dissipation is a function of ambient temperature, the maximum allowable junction
temperature and the thermal resistance from junction-to-air (i.e., T , T ,  ). Exceeding the maximum allowable power
A J JA
dissipation causes the device to initiate thermal shutdown. Please see Section5.0 “Thermal Considerations” for
more details.

20.text:
TC1263
4.0 DETAILED DESCRIPTION 4.2 ERROR Output
The TC1263 is a precision, fixed-output LDO. Unlike ERROR is driven low whenever V OUT falls out of
bipolar regulators, the TC1263’s supply current does regulation by more than – 5% (typ.). This condition may
not increase with load current. In addition, V be caused by low input voltage, output current limiting
OUT
remains stable and within regulation over the entire or thermal limiting. The ERROR threshold is 5% below
0mA to I LOADMAX load current range (an important rated V OUT , regardless of the programmed output
consideration in RTC and CMOS RAM battery back-up voltage value (e.g., ERROR = V OL at 4.75V (typ.) for a
applications). 5.0V regulator and 2.85V (typ.) for a 3.0V regulator).

4.1 Output Capacitor
VOL
A 1µF (min.) capacitor from V to ground is
OUT
required. The output capacitor should have an Effective FIGURE 4-2: ERROR Output Operation.
Series Eesistance (ESR) greater than 0.1 and less
than 5. A 1µF capacitor should be connected from
V to GND if there is either more than 10 inches of wire
IN
between the regulator and the AC filter capacitor or a
battery is used as the power source. Aluminum
electrolytic or tantalum capacitor types can be used.
Since many aluminum electrolytic capacitors freeze at
approximately -30°C, solid tantalums are
recommended for applications operating below -25°C.
When operating from sources other than batteries,
supply-noise rejection and transient response can be
improved by increasing the value of the input and
output capacitors, and by employing passive filtering
techniques.

Note the following details of the code protection feature on Microchip devices: • Microchip products meet the specification contained in their particular Microchip Data Sheet.
• Microchip believes that its family of products is one of the most secure families of its kind on the market today, when used in the intended manner and under normal conditions. • There are dishonest and possibly illegal methods used to breach the code protection feature. All of these methods, to our knowledge, require using the Microchip products in a manner outside the operating specifications contained in Microchip’s Data Sheets. Most likely, the person doing so is engaged in theft of intellectual property. • Microchip is willing to work with the customer who is concerned about the integrity of their code. • Neither Microchip nor any other semiconductor manufacturer can guarantee the security of their code. Code protection does not mean that we are guaranteeing the product as “unbreakable.” Code protection is constantly evolving. We at Microchip are committed to continuously improving the code protection features of our products. Attempts to break Microchip’s code protection feature may be a violation of the Digital Millennium Copyright Act. If such acts allow unauthorized access to your software or other copyrighted work, you may have a right to sue for relief under that Act.
